budget_wines.name: Leitz Eins Zwei Dry Riesling
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Weingut Leitz, Ruedesheim am Rhein; sold widely in Germany and exported to specialist retailers across Europe and the United States.
budget_wines.description: The entry dry Riesling from Johannes Leitz in Ruedesheim: bright citrus and stone fruit on slatey Rheingau terroir, with the off-dry house style locked into a fully trocken finish.
budget_wines.tip: Eins Zwei Dry is the easiest Leitz to find abroad and the cheapest way into a serious Ruedesheim grower's dry Rheingau Riesling.

budget_wines.name: Leitz Ruedesheimer Magdalenenkreuz Riesling
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Weingut Leitz, Ruedesheim am Rhein; sold through the estate and Rheingau specialists in Europe and North America.
budget_wines.description: A single-village dry Riesling from the Ruedesheimer Magdalenenkreuz site, sitting above the Eins Zwei in the Leitz range with more body and slate-and-citrus grip on the finish.
budget_wines.tip: Magdalenenkreuz is the value step up from Eins Zwei; an under-EUR-20 way to taste Leitz's Ruedesheim village character before stepping into the GG tier.

budget_wines.name: Robert Weil Tradition Riesling Trocken
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Weingut Robert Weil, Kiedrich; sold through the estate and Rheingau wine specialists across Germany and abroad.
budget_wines.description: The estate-base dry Riesling from a Kiedrich VDP estate, intended as the everyday Weil bottling, with crisp citrus, slate freshness and the polished house style at an approachable price.
budget_wines.tip: Tradition is the entry into Weil's Kiedrich estate and the cheapest way to taste the polished Weil signature before the Erste Lage and GG bottles.

budget_wines.name: Kuenstler Hochheimer Riesling Trocken
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Weingut Kuenstler, Hochheim am Main; sold through the estate vinothek and Rheingau specialists across Germany.
budget_wines.description: Gunter Kuenstler's village-level Hochheim Riesling: a dry, mineral, citrus-driven Rheingau from the warmer Main-side slopes, the wine that anchors the everyday Kuenstler tier.
budget_wines.tip: Kuenstler's Hochheimer Ortswein is the value pick from a serious VDP estate; pair it with smoked fish or roast pork to read its Hochheim weight.

budget_wines.name: Spreitzer 101 Riesling Trocken
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Weingut Josef Spreitzer, Oestrich-Winkel; sold through the estate vinothek and Rheingau-focused retailers in Europe and the United States.
budget_wines.description: The entry dry Riesling from the Spreitzer brothers in Oestrich-Winkel, named for the estate's 101 parcels: a clean, slatey, dry village Riesling at a friendly price.
budget_wines.tip: 101 trocken is the affordable taste of Spreitzer's modern Rheingau style before stepping into the Hendelberg and Eisenberg GGs.

budget_wines.name: Allendorf Rheingau Riesling Trocken
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Weingut Fritz Allendorf, Winkel; sold through the estate, Rheingau specialists and supermarket fine-wine sections across Germany.
budget_wines.description: Allendorf's entry dry Rheingau Riesling from Winkel: a fresh, citrus-and-apple, fully trocken everyday Riesling from the 75-hectare Allendorf VDP estate.
budget_wines.tip: Allendorf's entry trocken is widely stocked sub-EUR-15 Rheingau Riesling in German supermarkets and a reliable everyday Rheingau dry style.

budget_wines.name: Wegeler Geheimrat J Riesling Trocken
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Weingut Wegeler, Oestrich-Winkel; sold through the estate, the Wegeler group and Rheingau fine-wine retailers in Germany and abroad.
budget_wines.description: Wegeler's flagship dry Rheingau Riesling cuvee, blended from estate sites in Winkel, Oestrich and Geisenheim: a structured, polished trocken with citrus, peach and mineral length.
budget_wines.tip: Geheimrat J sits at the top of the budget tier; with three to five years of bottle age it shifts into a serious dry-Riesling drinking window.

budget_wines.name: Schloss Vollrads Volratz Riesling Trocken
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Schloss Vollrads, Oestrich-Winkel; sold through the estate and Rheingau retailers across Germany and through the estate's export distribution.
budget_wines.description: The Volratz entry trocken from Schloss Vollrads, a documented Rheingau wine estate active since the 13th century: a dry, fresh, accessible village-level Riesling at the bottom of the Vollrads range.
budget_wines.tip: Volratz is the cheapest way into Schloss Vollrads; visit the castle on the same trip to see the historic moated estate that produces it.

budget_wines.name: Kloster Eberbach Riesling Trocken
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Hessische Staatsweingueter Kloster Eberbach, Eltville; sold through the abbey shop and Rheingau retailers across Germany.
budget_wines.description: The estate dry Rheingau Riesling from the Hessische Staatsweingueter at Kloster Eberbach, an accessible trocken from the state-owned holdings that include the monopole Steinberg above the abbey.
budget_wines.tip: Buy a bottle on a Kloster Eberbach visit; the abbey shop carries the entry trocken and the single-site Steinberg side by side for context.

budget_wines.name: Schloss Johannisberg Gelblack Riesling Feinherb
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Schloss Johannisberg, Geisenheim; sold through the estate, the Henkell Freixenet wine portfolio and Rheingau retailers in Germany and abroad.
budget_wines.description: Schloss Johannisberg's colour-capsule Gelblack feinherb: an off-dry monopole Riesling from the south-facing Johannisberg slope, made in the historic Schloss Johannisberg house style.
budget_wines.tip: The Gelblack capsule signals feinherb at Schloss Johannisberg; serve it cool with smoked trout or pork belly to read its off-dry balance.

budget_wines.name: Peter Jakob Kuhn Jacobus Riesling Trocken
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Weingut Peter Jakob Kuhn, Oestrich-Winkel; sold through the estate and biodynamic specialist importers in Europe and North America.
budget_wines.description: The Jacobus dry Riesling from Peter Jakob Kuhn in Oestrich-Winkel, the Demeter-certified estate's entry trocken: a clean, low-intervention biodynamic Rheingau Riesling at a serious-estate entry price.
budget_wines.tip: Jacobus is the affordable taste of biodynamic Kuhn before the single-site GGs from the Doosberg and the Hendelberg.

budget_wines.name: August Eser Oestrich Riesling Trocken
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Weingut August Eser, Oestrich-Winkel; sold through the estate and organic / biodynamic specialists in Germany and abroad.
budget_wines.description: August Eser's Oestrich village dry Riesling, an entry trocken from an organic Rheingau estate with parcels in the Doosberg and Lenchen: clean, citrus-driven and fresh at an everyday price.
budget_wines.tip: Eser sits at the affordable end of certified-organic Rheingau; the Oestrich Ortswein is the gateway to the single-site Doosberg.

budget_wines.name: Querbach Rheingau Riesling Trocken
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Weingut Querbach, Oestrich-Winkel; sold through the estate and Rheingau specialists across Germany.
budget_wines.description: Peter Querbach's estate dry Riesling from Oestrich-Winkel: a low-intervention, hand-harvested, fully trocken Rheingau Riesling from an organic-leaning family estate.
budget_wines.tip: Querbach is a quiet insider name; the estate trocken is the entry bottle, served at room temperature shows its slate-and-citrus tension.

budget_wines.name: Joachim Flick Rheingau Riesling Trocken
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Weingut Flick, Floersheim-Wicker; sold through the estate vinothek and Rheingau specialists across Germany.
budget_wines.description: Joachim Flick's estate dry Rheingau Riesling from Floersheim-Wicker, the eastern edge of the appellation: a fresh, citrus-and-apple trocken from a family estate just east of Hochheim.
budget_wines.tip: Flick is the cheapest serious Wickerer Stein and Floersheimer Wicker Riesling on the market; the estate trocken is the place to start.
